reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom (sakura) spots in Tohoku but tool fetched generic Akita spots — no sakura-specific filtering. Results include Akita Castle and Lake Tazawa, not cherry-blossom landmarks. Kakunodate (a prime Tohoku sakura site) absent from truncated results shown. Only one prefecture covered; Tohoku spans six.
missing_data: Sakura-tagged spots (sakura_meisho_100 kind) from multiple Tohoku prefectures (Miyagi, Iwate, Yamagata, Aomori, Fukushima). Kakunodate weeping cherry, Hirosaki Castle, Kitakami Tenshochi are canonical omissions.
improvement_hint: Route query through a sakura-kind filter (kinds=sakura_meisho_100) across all Tohoku prefectures, or apply region fan-out to cover all six prefectures rather than only Akita.

reasoning: Tool correctly uses get_local_specialty for Niigata sake. Returns 24 items including the three famous 越の三梅 brands (越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田) with accurate Wikidata QIDs and descriptions. Minor issue: query asks for 'major breweries' but response mixes sake brands, GI items (edamame). Core sake data is accurate and comprehensive.
missing_data: Physical brewery visit addresses or tour availability not in data. More brewery-level entities beyond top 3 brand entries.
improvement_hint: Add a sake_brewery sub-filter so brewery entities rank above GI food items when query intent is specifically breweries; provide 'visit_info' field if available.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts WITH onsen in Hakuba. Tool returns Hakuba Happo-One (correct, 1998 Olympics venue) but no onsen entries. Municipal scrape results are noise (UN Tourism award notice). Onsen-tagged spots or hotels completely absent from truncated response. Dual-constraint (ski + onsen combo) not addressed.
missing_data: Onsen spots/hotels in Hakuba area (e.g. Happo-One onsen, Mimizuku-no-yu, Wadano Onsen). A get_hotels call for Hakuba with onsen_ryokan type would complement. Hakuba47 and other ski resorts absent too.
improvement_hint: For ski+onsen queries, fan out to both get_spots (ski_resort kind) and get_hotels (onsen_ryokan type) in the same municipality; surface the combo result explicitly.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage (koyo) spots in Kansai region but tool fetches generic Kyoto spots. Nijō Castle and Tō-ji are prominent but no foliage-specific tags. Kansai region spans more than Kyoto (Osaka, Nara, Hyogo, Shiga, Mie not covered). Leading results (Nagaoka-kyō, Izumi-ji) are generic heritage items, not koyo-specific.
missing_data: Spots tagged with autumn-foliage/koyo kind across all Kansai prefectures. Daigo-ji, Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji, Rurikoin are canonical koyo spots missing. Osaka/Nara/Shiga coverage absent.
improvement_hint: Apply kinds filter for foliage-related tags (e.g. koyo, momiji) and enable region fan-out across Kansai prefectures instead of single-prefecture call.

reasoning: Arabic query about famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool fetches Nara which is core Kansai and returns Yoshino-yama (sakura_meisho_100, prominence 1.1) as the first result — arguably Japan's most famous sakura site. Kōfuku-ji and other Nara classics follow. Query says 'Kansai' but single-prefecture fetch is partially satisfying since Yoshino is the standout answer.
missing_data: Coverage of Kyoto sakura spots (Maruyama, Arashiyama, Keage) and Osaka (Osaka Castle). Region-level fan-out would complete the picture.
improvement_hint: Expand to at least Kyoto and Osaka for Kansai sakura queries; or apply sakura_meisho_100 filter across all Kansai prefectures.

reasoning: Query specifically asks about stargazing (星空) on remote Okinawan islands (離島). Tool returns generic Okinawa spots — castles (Nakagusuku, Zakimi), royal tomb (Tamaudun), none related to stargazing or remote islands. Kumejima appears with nagisa_100 tag but no dark-sky or star-gazing tags. Core intent (dark sky +離島) completely unmet.
missing_data: Dark-sky / star-gazing tagged locations. Iriomote, Taketomi, Yonaguni, Miyakojima islands missing. No 'dark_sky' or astro-tourism kind in corpus for Okinawa remote islands.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky and astro_tourism kind tags to applicable remote Okinawa island entities; route stargazing queries to those kinds plus island kind with low crowd_band preference.

reasoning: Thai query about rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returns 秘湯 (hito-yu-kai) ryokan members: 知内温泉, 銀婚湯, 凌雲閣, 湧駒荘 — all are genuine remote/rural onsen inns confirmed via Nihon Hito-yu Association. Addresses provided. Partial noise: ANA Holiday Inn (Sapporo urban hotel) appears in truncated results but is minor ranking issue.
missing_data: Coordinates for hito_yu_kai entries are null, making geographic planning difficult. English names for rural ryokan absent.
improvement_hint: Geocode hito_yu_kai entries; add crowd_band=low filter preference for 'rural' queries to suppress urban chain hotels ranking above countryside ryokan.

reasoning: Vietnamese query about islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool fetches Kagawa spots returning Ritsurin Garden (mainland garden, excellent but not an island), Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle — none are islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Major omissions: Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima — the most visited Setouchi islands absent from truncated response.
missing_data: Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima, Inujima island spots. Island-kind (島) filtering absent. Hiroshima's Miyajima (Itsukushima) also part of Setouchi context.
improvement_hint: Route 'Seto Inland Sea islands' queries with kinds=island filter across Kagawa, Hiroshima, Ehime, Okayama prefectures; include Setouchi Triennale context.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Response returns METI craft data: 阿波正藍しじら織 (indigo-related textile) and 阿波和紙 (indigo-dyed paper), both relevant. However, no workshop/experience venue information included — only product category data. The practical 'where to experience dyeing' aspect is not addressed. Otani Ware (ceramics) is off-topic.
missing_data: Workshop/experience venues for indigo dyeing (体験工房). Specific studios offering hands-on 藍染 experiences in Tokushima. The 'workshop' in the query is experience-oriented but MCP lacks experience-venue data.
improvement_hint: Supplement craft tool with experience-venue entities (体験工房) tagged by craft kind; or add a see_also note directing to municipal scrape results for workshop bookings.

reasoning: German query about shukubo on Mt. Koya. Tool correctly uses get_hotels for Wakayama and returns multiple confirmed shukubo entries from 高野山宿坊協会 (Koyasan Shukubo Association): 安養院, 一乗院, 惠光院, 北室院, 熊谷寺. All are authentic, located in Koya-cho, with phone numbers and rich Japanese descriptions of spiritual experience. Perfectly matches intent.
missing_data: English names for shukubo entries; coordinates for navigation. Pricing/availability info absent but expected limitation.
improvement_hint: Add English translations for shukubo names and descriptions; geocode shukubo entries via Koya-san official coordinates.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area on 武家屋敷 returns 153 matches including Shimane 武家屋敷 (Matsue), Fukui 武家屋敷旧内山家, and the standout Kakunodate samurai district (Akita, buke_yashiki + sakura_meisho_100, prominent 0.95, description mentions weeping cherry). Good geographic spread. Minor: Tokyo entry appears (BUNKA-491) but that is arguably 'outside Kyoto'.
missing_data: Hagi (Yamaguchi) samurai district, Chiran (Kagoshima) samurai village — both are Important Preservation Districts. Addresses/opening hours for the non-wikidata entries.
improvement_hint: Boost buke_yashiki + preservation_district kind entities; explicitly filter or deprioritize entries lacking description_en to help the agent surface visitable sites.

reasoning: Query asks about summer festivals in Akita. Tool returns only 3 items (count=3) — all bunka_intangible (cultural property rituals), none explicitly summer outdoor festivals. The famous Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり, one of Japan's top three summer festivals) and Omagari Fireworks are absent from the festival list. Coverage critically sparse for the intent.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり) — not in bunka_intangible corpus. Omagari hanabi. Scraped schema events for Akita summer festivals. The 大曲の花火 exists as a Wikidata spot but doesn't appear in get_festivals output.
improvement_hint: Supplement bunka_intangible with scraped_schema_event results for Akita summer season; Kanto Festival should be in the corpus as a UNESCO candidate or major event listing.

reasoning: Korean query about small fishing port towns in San'in region. Tool returns generic Shimane spots: Lake Shinji (lake), Matsue Castle (castle), Iwami Ginzan (mine/UNESCO), Mount Sanbe (mountain) — none are fishing port towns. Nishi-Ogi (西ノ島), Saigo on Oki Islands, Hama-da port, or coastal fishing villages completely absent from shown results.
missing_data: Fishing village/port entities (漁港, 漁師町 kinds) in Shimane and Tottori. Oki Islands (隠岐) as a remote fishing destination. San'in Geopark coastal towns. Tottori prefecture not included (San'in covers both Shimane + Tottori).
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port and fishing_village kind tags to coastal municipalities; expand query to Tottori as well for complete San'in coverage; filter by coast/beach/harbor kinds.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. Tool returns Gifu crafts (8 items): Mino Ware, Hida Shunkei lacquer, Nagiso lathe work, Ichii Ittobori carving — but the specific washi paper item (美濃和紙, Mino Washi) is likely present but not visible in truncation. The 'experience' aspect (workshop visit) cannot be served by craft designation data alone.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi, METI designated craft) if not in the 8 items. Workshop/experience venue listings for washi making. Udatsu no Machitoki museum and washi experience centers in Mino City.
improvement_hint: Ensure 美濃和紙 appears prominently for washi queries; add experience-venue cross-link. The truncation prevents confirming washi is included — tool should prioritize intent-matching items.

reasoning: Spanish query about fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool fetches Ishikawa prefecture spots, returning Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine — all in Kanazawa city, far from Noto Peninsula. No Noto-specific results (Wajima, Suzu, Monzen) visible. Constraint to Noto Peninsula geography completely missed by prefecture-level fetch without municipality filtering.
missing_data: Wajima (輪島), Suzu (珠洲), Monzen, Noto-cho fishing villages. Wajima morning market, Shiroyone Senmaida terraced rice fields. Noto Peninsula Geopark coastal spots. 2024 earthquake context.
improvement_hint: Add municipality-level filtering for 'Noto Peninsula' queries (e.g. 輪島市, 珠洲市, 能登町); or implement a geographic sub-region concept for peninsulas.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area on 砂丘 returns 91 matches with good diversity: Tottori (expected but present), Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari dune museum (Hokkaido), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Tsujidō (Kanagawa). Geographic spread is good. Tottori appears prominently (first) which is expected even if not 'lesser-known', but other entries satisfy the 'not just Tottori' aspect.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka) — one of Japan's three great sand dunes. Sarobetsu sand dunes (Hokkaido). More named sand dune locations with descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add descriptions to sand_dune-tagged entries; the Ishikari entry is a museum about dunes, not a dune itself — distinguish physical dune from interpretation center.

reasoning: Chinese query about traditional crafts of San'in region. Tool correctly uses get_local_specialty for Shimane with craft category and Chinese language, returning 4 METI-designated items with accurate Chinese translations: 石見焼 (Iwami ware), 石州和紙 (Sekishu washi), 雲州そろばん (Unshū abacus), 出雲石燈ろう (Izumo stone lanterns). All are genuine Shimane crafts with high-confidence translations.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts (e.g. Tottori bamboo work) for full San'in coverage. Wikidata venue see_also section truncated before rendering.
improvement_hint: Expand San'in queries to include Tottori prefecture in addition to Shimane for complete regional coverage; enable region_fanout for San'in concept.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returns generic Hokkaido spots: Shōwa-shinzan (volcano), Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — none related to lavender. No lavender-tagged entities visible. Furano Farm Tomita and other lavender fields absent. The lavender kind exists in data but generic prefecture call doesn't surface them.
missing_data: Lavender farm entities: Farm Tomita (富田ファーム), Lavender East (東大雪ラベンダー) in Nukabira, Hokuryu sunflower/lavender fields. Spots with kinds=lavender_farm or flower_garden in Hokkaido.
improvement_hint: Route lavender queries to kinds=lavender_farm or flower_garden filter; beyond Furano intent should deprioritize Furano entries and surface other Hokkaido lavender locations.

reasoning: Filipino query about traditional towns (古い町並み) in Kyushu — possibly mistranslated as 'traditional coffee towns' but intent seems to be old townscapes. Tool fetches Oita spots: Usuki Castle, Usa Jingū, Oka Castle, Kokonoe Town — none are traditional townscape (machiya/komachi) preservation districts. Kyushu-wide coverage absent. Yanagawa, Hita, Uchiko (Ehime border) type machitami not present.
missing_data: Preservation districts in Kyushu: Hita Mameda-machi (Oita), Yanagawa canal town, Nagasaki Dejima, Kagoshima old districts. If intent is 'old townscapes' then kominka/preservation_district kind filter needed.
improvement_hint: Route 'traditional town' / machitami queries to preservation_district + jokamachi kinds; expand to multi-prefecture Kyushu if old townscape is the intent.

reasoning: Query asks about staying in kominka (old folk houses) in Hokuriku. Tool uses get_hotels for Ishikawa and returns onsen ryokan (秘湯) and generic hotels — none are kominka-type lodging. A 'Student housing 1' entry (name_en) appears — likely an OSM misclassification and potentially a hallucination-adjacent noise item. Fukui/Toyama (also Hokuriku) not covered.
missing_data: Kominka-stay lodging (古民家宿) entities in Ishikawa, Fukui, Toyama. Shirakawa-go area gassho-zukuri farmhouse stays. Lodging type=kominka completely absent from hotel corpus.
wrong_data: 'Student housing 1' appearing as a hotel result is an OSM data quality issue — not a true lodging recommendation.
improvement_hint: Add kominka lodging type to hotel taxonomy; scrape/integrate Satoyama Experience and similar kominka stay registries; filter out OSM nodes with 'student housing' classification from tourism hotel results.

reasoning: Query about mountain shrines on Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage for Wakayama returns 7 Japan Heritage stories and top_canonical_heritage including Kongōbu-ji (Koya-san), Kumano Kodō (pilgrimage route, UNESCO WHS), Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines, UNESCO WHS). The tool_scope_note correctly redirects for UNESCO/pilgrimage context. Highly relevant and accurate mountain-religious landscape data.
missing_data: Nachi Taisha and Hongū Taisha as individual spot entries with coordinates. Yoshino-Kumano National Park mountain shrine context. Trekking access information.
improvement_hint: Surface individual Kumano Sanzan shrine entries (Hongū, Nachi, Hayatama) as separate results with coordinates; Kongōbu-ji has a heritage_designations label 'Q11633166' unresolved — should be labeled properly.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along Sea of Japan coast. Tool gets Niigata festivals (129 items) and includes 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks) — Japan's premier fireworks event, correct and directly relevant. However it's ranked 3rd after a May flower festival and a lily festival. Hanabi content is present but buried under non-fireworks events. Sea-of-Japan coast scope (Akita, Ishikawa, Fukui, Yamagata) not covered.
missing_data: Omagari fireworks (Akita), Kashiwazaki fireworks, other Sea of Japan coast fireworks events. Multi-prefecture coverage for the full Sea of Japan coast.
improvement_hint: Apply fireworks/hanabi kind filter when seasonal summer + 花火 intent detected; fan out to all Sea of Japan coast prefectures; rank hanabi events above generic spring festivals for this query.

reasoning: Query about traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool correctly uses get_local_specialty for Oita crafts and returns 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts, METI designated) with accurate description covering Madake bamboo, 8 weaving techniques, production areas (Beppu, Hita, Yufu). Hakata Ori also appears as a multi-prefecture craft that includes Oita. see_also venues are museums, which is reasonable supplementary data.
missing_data: Workshop/experience venues for bamboo craft in Beppu. More detail on visiting Beppu Bamboo Craft Institute (竹細工伝統産業会館).
improvement_hint: Link craft records to known experience venues or 伝統産業会館 entities when available; these would directly answer 'where to see/try' follow-up questions.

reasoning: Query about cycling routes in rural Shikoku. Tool fetches Ehime spots. Ōmishima island (cycling_route + walking_road_500 kinds, 4-hour visit) directly relevant. However it's listed 3rd after Ōzu Castle and Yuzuki Castle. The Shimanami Kaidō (Japan's premier cycling route connecting Honshu-Shikoku via Seto Inland Sea islands) is not explicitly named in shown results, though Ōmishima is part of it. Rural Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa cycling spots absent.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaidō as a named cycling route entity. Cycling-specific spots in Kochi (Shimanto River cycling), Tokushima (Yoshino River). Multi-prefecture Shikoku coverage.
improvement_hint: Create a Shimanami Kaidō route entity with cycling_route kind; apply kinds=cycling_route filter across all Shikoku prefectures for cycling-intent queries; expand beyond single prefecture.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returns 183 items with kabuki (UNESCO ICH Q199701) correctly present at rank 2. However the list begins with Bunraku (puppet theatre) and other unrelated arts (Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura). The 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' geographic constraint is not applied. Venue-specific information (Ōsaka Shochikuza, Kotohira-za in Kagawa, Uchiko-za in Ehime) absent.
missing_data: Kabuki performance venue entities: Kotohira-za (Kagawa), Uchiko-za (Ehime), Kanamaru-za (Kagawa) — the oldest surviving kabuki theaters. Geographic filtering to exclude Tokyo/Kyoto not applied.
improvement_hint: Add kabuki_theater kind to venue entities; when kabuki intent detected with location exclusion, surface kabuki_theater-tagged spots outside major metros; link to the kabuki art record from venue data.
